title: IgE-Mediated Peanut Allergy: Mechanisms and Clinical Outcomes
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: This study investigates the immunological mechanisms underlying IgE-mediated peanut allergy in pediatric patients. We analysed 342 patients over 5 years and identified key biomarkers predictive of severe anaphylactic reactions. Results suggest that early allergen exposure protocols may reduce sensitisation rates by 40%.

title: Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis and Food Allergy Susceptibility in Infants
abstract: Prospective cohort study of 1,200 infants from birth to 36 months examining the relationship between gut microbiome composition and development of food allergies. Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium abundance at 3 months predicted 73% lower food allergy risk at age 3.

title: Sublingual Immunotherapy Efficacy for House Dust Mite Allergy
abstract: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 42 randomised trials evaluating sublingual immunotherapy for house dust mite allergy. Significant reductions in rhinitis symptom scores (SMD -0.68) and rescue medication use observed across all age groups.

title: Allergic Asthma Phenotyping Using Machine Learning: A Multiomics Approach
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Integration of genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics data from 2,800 asthmatic patients to identify distinct allergic asthma endotypes. Five phenotypic clusters identified with differential treatment response profiles, enabling precision medicine approaches.

title: Immunological Basis of Latex Allergy in Healthcare Workers
abstract: Cross-sectional study examining IgE sensitisation patterns to latex proteins in 1,500 healthcare workers. Hev b 5 and Hev b 13 identified as dominant allergen components. Powdered glove exposure correlated with 3.4-fold increased sensitisation risk.

title: Wheat-Dependent Exercise-Induced Anaphylaxis: Case Series and Review
abstract: Case series of 78 patients with wheat-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis presenting over 10 years. All confirmed omega-5-gliadin sensitisation. Cofactors including NSAIDs and alcohol significantly lowered reaction thresholds.

title: Early Introduction of Allergenic Foods: LEAP Trial 5-Year Follow-up
abstract: Five-year follow-up of the Learning Early About Peanut (LEAP) trial. Children who consumed peanuts early maintained tolerance through school age. Sustained unresponsiveness rate of 74% in early-introduction group vs 19% in avoidance group at 72 months.

title: Cockroach Allergen Sensitisation in Inner-City Children with Asthma
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Inner City Asthma Consortium study examining Bla g 2 (cockroach allergen) sensitisation in 1,100 inner-city children with persistent asthma. High bedroom allergen levels associated with increased ER visits, oral steroid use, and school absences.

title: Climate Change and Allergen Seasonality Shift: 30-Year Analysis
abstract: Analysis of 30-year pollen monitoring data from 173 stations demonstrating significant advances in pollen season onset (average 20 days earlier) and increased total pollen loads (46% increase) attributable to rising CO2 and temperature. Ragweed season extended by 25 days in northern latitudes.

title: Dupilumab for Severe Pediatric Atopic Dermatitis: Long-Term Safety Data
journal: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology
abstract: Three-year open-label extension study of dupilumab in children aged 6 months to 5 years with severe atopic dermatitis. Consistent EASI-75 achievement (89%) maintained throughout. No new safety signals; conjunctivitis rate 5.2%, injection site reactions 8.1%.

title: Allergen Avoidance in Pregnancy and Offspring Allergy Risk: Systematic Review
abstract: Systematic review of 56 studies examining whether maternal allergen avoidance during pregnancy reduces offspring allergy. Meta-analysis finds no protective effect of avoidance diets; some evidence suggests diverse maternal diet reduces offspring sensitisation by 23%.

title: Nasal Polyposis and CRS with Eosinophilia: Surgical vs. Biologic Outcomes
abstract: Head-to-head comparison of endoscopic sinus surgery versus biologics (dupilumab, mepolizumab) in 400 patients with CRSwNP over 52 weeks. Biologics superior on SNOT-22 scores; surgery superior on objective endoscopy scores. Combination approach showed best overall outcomes.

title: Alpha-Gal Syndrome: Tick Bite-Induced Mammalian Meat Allergy
abstract: Prospective study of 890 patients with delayed anaphylaxis to mammalian meat confirmed as alpha-gal syndrome following Amblyomma americanum tick bites. Increasing incidence correlated with tick geographic expansion. All patients showed specific IgE to galactose-alpha-1,3-galactose.

title: Natural History of Egg Allergy in a Prospective Pediatric Cohort
abstract: Longitudinal follow-up of 620 egg-allergic children from diagnosis to age 16. Resolution rate of 68% by age 12; Gal d 1 (ovomucoid) sensitisation at diagnosis was the strongest predictor of persistence. Baked egg tolerance acquisition preceded raw egg tolerance in 88% of resolvers.

title: Cashew and Tree Nut Cross-Reactivity: Component-Level Analysis
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology: In Practice
abstract: Component-resolved analysis of 340 tree nut-allergic patients to characterise cross-reactivity between cashew, pistachio, walnut, and hazelnut. Ana o 3 (cashew 2S albumin) IgE levels correlated strongly with reaction severity. Cross-reactivity between cashew and pistachio confirmed at 78% of cases.

title: Food Protein-Induced Enterocolitis Syndrome (FPIES): Diagnostic Criteria Validation
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Multicentre validation study of the 2017 International FPIES consensus diagnostic criteria across 18 paediatric centres. Criteria showed 94% sensitivity and 87% specificity when applied prospectively. Solid food FPIES (rice, oat) increasingly recognised in older children.

title: Aspirin-Exacerbated Respiratory Disease: Phenotype Characterisation in 1,500 Patients
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Comprehensive phenotypic analysis of 1,500 patients with AERD across 12 tertiary referral centres. Strong correlation between arachidonic acid pathway dysregulation and clinical severity. Aspirin desensitisation resulted in 52% improvement in nasal polyp burden at 12 months.

title: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome: Revised Diagnostic Criteria and Clinical Features
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Revised consensus criteria for mast cell activation syndrome, incorporating updated tryptase kinetics and new minor criteria. Retrospective validation in 560 patients distinguishing primary, secondary, and idiopathic MCAS subtypes with distinct treatment implications.

title: The Allergic March: Birth Cohort Analysis from BAMSE Study
abstract: 24-year follow-up of the BAMSE birth cohort (2,033 children). Classic atopic march sequence confirmed in 38% of allergic children. Filaggrin loss-of-function variants and early sensitisation to food allergens were the strongest predictors of march progression from eczema to asthma.

title: Birch Pollen-Related Food Allergy Syndrome in Northern Europe
abstract: Multicentre study of 1,800 birch pollen-allergic patients across Scandinavia and Germany characterising pollen-food allergy syndrome. Bet v 1-homologous proteins in apple, hazelnut, carrot, and celery accounted for 91% of reactions. Immunotherapy to birch pollen alleviated food symptoms in 67% of treated patients.

title: Cat Allergen Fel d 1 Sensitisation and Asthma Risk: A Meta-Analysis
abstract: Meta-analysis of 38 studies (n=41,200) examining Fel d 1 sensitisation as a risk factor for asthma development. Sensitisation increased asthma risk OR 3.2 (95% CI 2.6–3.9). High-level cat exposure without sensitisation showed a paradoxical protective effect in three Nordic cohorts.

title: Eosinophilic Esophagitis: Molecular Subtypes and Dietary Therapy Response
abstract: Transcriptomic analysis of oesophageal biopsies from 290 EoE patients identifies three molecular subtypes with distinct dietary trigger profiles. Subtype B responds preferentially to milk elimination; Subtype C requires six-food elimination for remission.

title: Tryptase as a Biomarker in Anaphylaxis and Mast Cell Disorders
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Review of tryptase as a diagnostic and prognostic biomarker across anaphylaxis, mastocytosis, and hereditary alpha-tryptasemia (HαT). Baseline tryptase above 11.4 ng/mL independently predicts severe anaphylaxis risk. HαT detected in 4–6% of the general population warrants routine screening.

title: TSLP Blockade with Tezepelumab in Severe Uncontrolled Asthma: NAVIGATOR Trial
abstract: NAVIGATOR phase III trial: tezepelumab (anti-TSLP) significantly reduced annual asthma exacerbation rates by 70% vs placebo in 1,061 adults with severe uncontrolled asthma, regardless of blood eosinophil count. First biologic effective across all inflammatory phenotypes.

title: Skin Barrier Dysfunction and Atopic Disease Progression: Filaggrin Revisited
abstract: Comprehensive review of filaggrin biology and its role in cutaneous allergen sensitisation and progression to systemic allergy. Novel FLG mutations identified in East Asian populations expand mutation spectrum. Skin barrier restoration strategies using emollient therapy show promise in preventing atopic march.

title: Microbiome Intervention with Lactobacillus reuteri in At-Risk Infants
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Randomised trial of Lactobacillus reuteri DSM 17938 supplementation in 232 at-risk infants from birth to 12 months. Probiotic group showed 40% reduction in IgE sensitisation rates and 32% reduction in eczema incidence at 24 months. Mechanism linked to TGF-β induction and Treg expansion.

title: Cefazolin and Perioperative Hypersensitivity: Risk Stratification and Management
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology: In Practice
abstract: Retrospective analysis of 210 perioperative anaphylaxis cases attributable to cefazolin across 8 surgical centres. Cefazolin-specific IgE testing showed 89% positive predictive value. Risk stratification algorithm proposed to guide preoperative evaluation in penicillin-labelled patients.

title: Soy Allergy in Peanut-Allergic Children: Prevalence and Cross-Reactive Epitopes
abstract: Multi-centre cohort study assessing true soy allergy prevalence among 880 peanut-allergic children. Confirmed soy allergy in 18%; most soy sensitisation non-clinical. Shared epitopes between Gly m 8 (soy) and Ara h 3 (peanut) explain partial co-sensitisation. Soy avoidance rarely needed without confirmed reactivity.

title: Oral Food Challenge Safety in Real-World Allergy Practice: 10-Year Review
abstract: Safety analysis of 12,400 oral food challenges performed across 22 allergy centres over 10 years. Severe systemic reaction rate of 0.08%; no fatalities. Challenges performed in specialist centres with trained staff carried a 6-fold lower risk of severe reactions compared to non-specialist settings.

title: Atopic Dermatitis Quality of Life: Validation of the EQ-5D-5L
abstract: Validation study of EQ-5D-5L as a preference-based quality of life measure in 2,100 adult atopic dermatitis patients. Psychometric analysis confirmed construct validity, responsiveness, and minimal clinically important difference of 0.07. Moderate-to-severe AD associated with utility values comparable to chronic kidney disease.

title: IL-33 and ST2 Pathway Regulation in Allergic Airway Disease
abstract: Mechanistic study identifying ST2 splice variants as modulators of IL-33-driven type 2 inflammation in murine and human allergic airway disease. Soluble ST2 acts as a decoy receptor; its ratio to membrane ST2 determines disease severity. Itepekimab (anti-IL-33) phase III data supports pathway targeting.

title: Mugwort Pollen and Celery-Carrot-Spice Cross-Reactivity Syndrome
abstract: Analysis of 580 mugwort-sensitised patients in Central Europe characterising the mugwort-celery-carrot-spice syndrome. Art v 1 and Art v 3 identified as primary sensitising molecules. Spice allergies (coriander, pepper, anise) occur in 34% of mugwort-sensitised patients. Season-specific exacerbation patterns documented.

title: Social Determinants of Health and Pediatric Asthma Disparities
abstract: National cohort analysis examining the contribution of social determinants (housing quality, neighbourhood pollution, food insecurity, healthcare access) to racial and socioeconomic disparities in pediatric asthma morbidity. Structural factors explained 71% of the disparity in asthma hospitalisations between Black and White children.

title: Telemedicine Allergy Consultations: Non-Inferiority Randomised Trial
abstract: Non-inferiority RCT comparing telemedicine vs in-person allergy consultations for follow-up of established patients (n=480). Primary endpoint: Allergy Control Score at 12 weeks. Telemedicine non-inferior with equivalent patient satisfaction scores. Travel time savings averaged 127 minutes per visit.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Allergy Diagnostics: A Systematic Scoping Review
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Scoping review of 82 AI applications in allergy and immunology. AI tools demonstrated accuracy of 87–94% for atopic dermatitis severity scoring, 91% for anaphylaxis trigger identification from EHR data, and 85% for allergen extract standardisation. Significant heterogeneity in validation approaches identified as a key gap.

title: Drug Allergy Delabeling: Outcomes in 2,000 Penicillin-Labelled Patients
abstract: Prospective evaluation of penicillin allergy delabeling protocol across 14 hospitals. 95.2% of penicillin-labelled patients tolerated direct amoxicillin challenge without prior skin testing. Programme reduced carbapenem use by 31% and C. difficile infections by 18%.

title: Radiocontrast Media Hypersensitivity: Premedication Protocols Reassessed
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology: In Practice
abstract: Retrospective cohort comparing corticosteroid ± antihistamine premedication regimens in 5,200 patients with prior radiocontrast hypersensitivity. 13-hour regimen no more effective than 4-hour regimen (breakthrough rate 4.2% vs 4.5%). Suggests simplified protocols are sufficient for most patients.

title: Nickel Contact Allergy in the Body Piercing Era: Epidemiological Shifts
abstract: Population-based analysis from the Danish Contact Dermatitis Database (n=18,500) demonstrating continued high nickel sensitisation prevalence (18.4% of women, 5.2% of men) despite EU nickel regulation. Body piercing remains a key sensitisation event. Digital device exposure identified as emerging nickel source.

title: Thunderstorm Asthma: Analysis of the Melbourne 2016 Epidemic
abstract: Detailed epidemiological and immunological analysis of the November 2016 Melbourne thunderstorm asthma epidemic that caused 10 deaths and 3,365 emergency presentations. Rye grass pollen rupture releasing respirable starch granules was confirmed as the mechanism. Risk model for future epidemic prediction developed.

title: Hereditary Angioedema Type I and II: Genotype-Phenotype Correlations
abstract: Largest genotype-phenotype correlation study in HAE to date (n=1,420, 380 distinct SERPING1 variants). Null mutations associated with lower C1-INH levels and earlier symptom onset compared to missense variants. Missense variants in the serine protease domain confer the most severe phenotype.

title: Oral Immunotherapy for Milk Allergy: Phase III Clinical Trial Results
abstract: Phase III randomised controlled trial evaluating the efficacy and safety of graduated oral immunotherapy in 580 milk-allergic patients aged 4–17. Primary endpoint: sustained unresponsiveness at 52 weeks. 67% of treated patients achieved desensitisation vs 4% placebo.

title: Anaphylaxis Management Guidelines Update 2024
abstract: Updated clinical practice guidelines for anaphylaxis recognition and emergency management. Key changes include revised epinephrine dosing protocols, updated criteria for biphasic reactions, and new recommendations for post-anaphylaxis observation periods in outpatient settings.

title: Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis: Diagnostic Criteria Revision 2022
authors: International Society for Human and Animal Mycology
abstract: Expert consensus report revising diagnostic criteria for allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis. New ISHAM-ABPA working group criteria introduced with simplified scoring. Validated against 312 confirmed cases across 8 international centres.

title: Biologics in Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria: Real-World Evidence
abstract: Real-world evidence report from 3,800 patients with chronic spontaneous urticaria treated with omalizumab. Complete response (UAS7=0) achieved in 52% at 6 months. Predictors of non-response identified including high baseline D-dimer and autoimmune comorbidities.

title: Venom Immunotherapy Outcomes: 10-Year Registry Analysis
abstract: Ten-year longitudinal registry data from 4,200 patients receiving venom immunotherapy for Hymenoptera allergy across 22 European centres. Complete protection rate of 96% for wasp venom; 88% for bee venom. Side-effect profile favourable across all age groups and venom types.

title: GINA Global Asthma Report 2024
abstract: Annual update of the GINA strategy document incorporating latest evidence. Key updates: biologic selection guidance expanded, low-dose ICS-formoterol as reliever updated for Steps 1–2, new sections on occupational asthma, and updated definition separating allergic from non-allergic phenotypes.

title: FDA Approval Report: Tezepelumab (Tezspire) for Severe Asthma
abstract: FDA approval summary for tezepelumab (Tezspire) as add-on maintenance treatment for severe uncontrolled asthma in adults and adolescents ≥12 years. Approval based on NAVIGATOR trial data. First approved biologic effective across all eosinophilic and non-eosinophilic asthma phenotypes.

title: EMA Assessment Report: Dupilumab Paediatric Label Extension
abstract: EMA Committee for Medicinal Products scientific assessment supporting dupilumab label extension to children aged 6 months to 5 years with severe atopic dermatitis. Based on LIBERTY AD PRESCHOOL trial. Benefit-risk profile considered positive given unmet medical need in this age group.

title: WHO Global Allergic Disease Burden Report 2024
abstract: Comprehensive WHO report on the global burden of allergic disease. Estimated 400 million people with allergic rhinitis, 300 million with asthma, 230 million with food allergy worldwide. Total economic burden estimated at USD 1.2 trillion annually including healthcare costs and productivity losses.

title: ARIA Digital Guidelines: Allergic Rhinitis Management 2024 Update
abstract: 2024 update of the Allergic Rhinitis and its Impact on Asthma (ARIA) guidelines, incorporating digital health tools and mHealth integration. Revised stepwise management algorithm; new section on immunotherapy decision support. Guideline adapted for low- and middle-income country contexts.

title: Practice Parameters: Drug Hypersensitivity Reactions 2022
authors: Joint Task Force on Practice Parameters
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology: In Practice
abstract: Updated joint practice parameters for diagnosis and management of drug hypersensitivity reactions. New sections on non-immediate reactions, DRESS syndrome, SJS/TEN, and management of COVID-19 vaccine hypersensitivity. Grading system for reaction severity aligned with WAO anaphylaxis grading.

title: ACE Inhibitor-Induced Angioedema: Hospital Protocol and Outcome Report
journal: Journal of the European Academy of Dermatology and Venereology
abstract: Prospective registry of 780 ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema admissions across 14 European hospitals. Icatibant shortened episode duration from median 32h (standard care) to 8h. Protocol using early icatibant reduced ICU admission rate from 18% to 4%. Mandatory ACE inhibitor discontinuation and patient counselling outcomes tracked.

title: Real-World Evidence: Mepolizumab in UK Clinical Practice
abstract: Two-year real-world evidence report from the UK Difficult Asthma Registry tracking 1,450 patients on mepolizumab following NICE approval. Exacerbation rate reduction of 58%, oral corticosteroid reduction of 75%, and NICE cost-effectiveness threshold met at GBP 22,400 per QALY. Responder characteristics defined.

title: HEAF Registry Annual Report: Hereditary Angioedema Outcomes
abstract: Third annual HEAF registry report covering 2,800 patients across 28 countries. Lanadelumab prophylaxis reduced attack rate by 87% from baseline. Access disparities between high- and low-income countries remain pronounced; 43% of patients in middle-income countries lack access to on-demand therapy.

title: Biologic Therapy Access Report: Healthcare Disparities in Allergy
authors: American College of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology
abstract: National survey of 680 allergists examining barriers to biologic therapy access. Insurance prior authorisation denial rates of 42% for dupilumab and 38% for mepolizumab on first submission. Patients from lower socioeconomic quintiles had 3.1-fold lower biologic initiation rates despite equivalent disease severity.

title: Allergen Immunotherapy Product Quality Standards: European Report
abstract: Joint EMA/Paul Ehrlich Institut report on allergen immunotherapy product quality standards across the European Union. Harmonised potency testing requirements proposed. Biological calibration using International Standards for 14 allergen extracts recommended. Gap analysis against FDA and WHO standards included.

title: Occupational Asthma: Compensation Claims and Healthcare Utilisation Analysis
abstract: Analysis of 12,000 occupational asthma compensation claims in the UK over 15 years. Isocyanates, flour dust, and animal dander remain top causative agents. Time from first symptom to correct diagnosis averaged 4.2 years, delaying removal from exposure and worsening long-term prognosis.

title: Food Allergy Labelling Legislation Compliance Audit: EU and US
abstract: Compliance audit of major allergen labelling across 4,200 processed food products in EU and US markets. Non-compliance rate of 7.2% in EU (Regulation 1169/2011) and 5.8% in US (FALCPA). Sesame, the newest US mandatory allergen, had the highest non-compliance rate at 18.4% in year one of enforcement.

title: National Allergy Strategy: Implementation Progress Report 2024
abstract: Fourth annual progress report on the Australian National Allergy Strategy 2019–2025. Metrics show 28% increase in GP allergy education completion, 15% improvement in adrenaline autoinjector prescription rates, and establishment of 6 new regional allergy centres. Workforce shortage in paediatric allergy remains a critical gap.

title: Subcutaneous Allergen Immunotherapy: Standardisation and Safety Protocol Report
authors: American Academy of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology: In Practice
abstract: AAAAI task force report establishing minimum safety standards for subcutaneous allergen immunotherapy. Mandated 20-30 minute post-injection observation period reaffirmed. Electronic medical record integration for dose escalation safety checks recommended. Systemic reaction reporting and quality improvement framework proposed.

title: Allergic Rhinitis Prevalence in Urban Populations 2020–2024
abstract: Longitudinal dataset tracking allergic rhinitis incidence across 18 major cities worldwide from 2020 to 2024. Includes patient demographics, pollen counts, pollution indices, and symptom severity scores. Raw dataset for meta-analysis use.

title: Airborne Pollen Monitoring Network: 2023 Annual Dataset
abstract: Complete dataset from 120 pollen monitoring stations across Europe for the 2023 season. Includes daily grain counts for birch, grass, olive, and ragweed. Correlated with hospital admission data for allergic rhinoconjunctivitis.

title: Contact Dermatitis Allergen Database 2024 Update
abstract: Updated structured database of 1,847 contact allergens with patch test concentrations, vehicles, prevalence rates, and cross-reactivity profiles. Includes newly identified sensitisers from cosmetics, industrial chemicals, and medical devices.

title: European Venom Allergy Registry: Raw Outcomes Dataset
abstract: Ten-year longitudinal raw dataset from 4,200 patients receiving venom immunotherapy across 22 European centres. Dataset includes sting challenge outcomes, maintenance dose adherence records, systemic reactions, and long-term protection rates.

title: Mediterranean Molecular Allergen Sensitisation Dataset
abstract: Component-resolved diagnostic dataset from 6,800 patients across Spain, Italy, Greece, and Portugal. IgE reactivity profiles to 112 recombinant and natural allergen molecules. Raw dataset enabling geographic sensitisation pattern analysis.

title: Fish and Shellfish Cross-Reactivity IgE Mapping Dataset
abstract: Cross-reactivity dataset mapping IgE binding across 38 fish species and 22 shellfish species using ImmunoCAP and ISAC arrays. Parvalbumin sequence homology database and clinical reaction cross-reference for 890 seafood-allergic patients.

title: Multi-Centre Skin Prick Test Reference Range Database
abstract: Standardised skin prick test reference range database compiled from 28,000 non-atopic controls across 15 countries. Wheal size reference distributions for 42 common inhalant and food allergens. Data stratified by age, sex, ethnicity, and device. Suitable for SPT interpretation guideline development.

title: ISAC 112 Molecular Allergen IgE Profiling: 10,000 Patient Dataset
abstract: De-identified raw ISAC microarray results for 10,000 patients from 9 countries tested with the ALEX2/ISAC 112 allergen panel. Includes clinical diagnosis, demographics, and concurrent skin prick test results. Largest publicly released molecular allergen dataset for machine learning and epidemiological research.

title: Birth Cohort Cord Blood IgE and 10-Year Allergy Outcome Data
abstract: Longitudinal dataset from the PREVASC multi-site birth cohort: cord blood IgE, microbiome samples at 1, 3, 6, and 12 months, and allergy outcomes (sensitisation, clinical diagnosis) at 1, 3, 5, and 10 years for 4,800 children. Includes dietary, environmental, and socioeconomic covariates.

title: Basophil Activation Test Reference Ranges and Validation Dataset
abstract: Standardisation dataset for basophil activation test (BAT) across 12 European laboratories. Reference ranges for CD63 and CD203c upregulation in response to 18 allergen stimuli established. Inter-laboratory coefficient of variation reduced from 34% to 9% after protocol harmonisation.

title: Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Food Challenge Outcomes Database
abstract: Pooled dataset of 5,200 DBPCFC results from 18 centres covering peanut, milk, egg, wheat, hazelnut, and fish. Includes eliciting doses, reaction severity, and objective/subjective symptoms. Dose-distribution modelling data for population-level allergen risk assessment and labelling threshold derivation.

title: Patient-Reported Outcomes in Atopic Dermatitis: PROSPECT Registry Data
abstract: De-identified registry dataset from the PROSPECT AD registry: 8,200 adult patients with atopic dermatitis across 45 dermatology centres. Quarterly POEM, DLQI, HADS, and VAS itch/sleep disturbance scores over 5 years with treatment history and flare records. Suitable for longitudinal PRO research.

title: Air Quality and Pollen Co-Exposure Dataset: 50 European Cities
abstract: Hourly air quality (PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3) and pollen concentration data from 50 European cities across 5 years. Linked with hospital emergency data for respiratory admissions. Designed to study synergistic effects of air pollutants and airborne allergens on allergic disease exacerbation.

title: Electronic Health Record Allergy Coding Accuracy Dataset
abstract: Audit dataset examining accuracy of allergy and adverse reaction coding in 2.1 million NHS patient records against SNOMED CT. Incorrect drug allergy codes found in 31% of records; penicillin allergy over-documentation rate of 89% confirmed. Dataset supports EHR allergy informatics research.

title: Food Allergen Labelling Audit Raw Data: Supermarket Survey 2024
abstract: Raw audit data from 6,100 packaged food products across 12 supermarket chains in UK, Germany, France, and Spain. Records mandatory allergen declarations, precautionary allergen labelling (PAL), ingredient list accuracy, and front-of-pack allergen summaries. Includes product category, country, and retailer.

title: Allergy Specialist Workforce Survey Data: Global 2023
abstract: Survey data from 4,200 allergists/immunologists across 68 countries on workforce capacity, training pathways, remuneration, and access to diagnostic tools. Dataset supports national workforce planning and identifies critical workforce shortages in sub-Saharan Africa, South-East Asia, and parts of Latin America.

title: Insurance Claims Analysis: Biologic Allergy Treatment Costs 2019–2023
abstract: Analysis of 2.8 million insurance claims for biologic allergy treatments (dupilumab, omalizumab, mepolizumab, benralizumab) from 2019 to 2023. Includes approval rates, denial rates, appeal outcomes, time-to-treatment, and total healthcare costs. Anonymised dataset for health economics research.

title: Paediatric Anaphylaxis Emergency Registry: 5-Year Dataset
abstract: Prospective emergency department registry of 3,800 paediatric anaphylaxis presentations across 42 paediatric emergency departments in 8 countries. Includes trigger, severity grade, time to epinephrine, biphasic reaction incidence, and discharge management. Harmonised data dictionary applied across all sites.

title: Allergy Disease Biobank: Sample Catalogue and Clinical Annotations
abstract: Catalogue of 120,000 biobanked samples (serum, PBMCs, skin biopsies, nasal lavage) from 28,000 patients with allergic diseases across 18 European centres. Linked clinical annotations include diagnosis, severity scores, treatment history, and genetic data. Access via consortium data access committee.

title: National School Food Allergy Accommodation Dataset: UK 2022–2024
abstract: Dataset covering food allergy accommodation in 12,400 UK primary and secondary schools. Records presence of trained staff, stock adrenaline availability, individual healthcare plan (IHP) completeness, and reported reactions. Baseline data from 2022 and post-intervention follow-up in 2024.

title: Novel Biologic Therapies Targeting IL-4/IL-13 in Atopic Dermatitis
abstract: Keynote presentation reviewing the clinical landscape of IL-4/IL-13 targeted biologics for moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis. Includes dupilumab, tralokinumab, and emerging pipeline agents with comparative efficacy data from head-to-head trials.

title: Allergen Immunotherapy Safety Monitoring Symposium
abstract: Symposium slides reviewing post-market surveillance data on subcutaneous allergen immunotherapy safety across 48 allergy practices. Systemic reaction rate of 0.1% per injection. New risk stratification algorithm proposed for pre-injection assessment.

title: Paediatric Anaphylaxis in School Settings: Prevention Protocols
abstract: Comprehensive slide deck covering current evidence and recommendations for anaphylaxis preparedness in K-12 school settings. Includes emergency action plan templates, stock epinephrine legislation overview, and nurse training protocols.

title: Biomarkers of Allergic Inflammation: ILC2 and Type 2 Cytokines
abstract: Review slides examining ILC2 biology and type 2 cytokine networks as biomarkers and therapeutic targets in allergic disease. Covers IL-33, TSLP, IL-25 alarmins and downstream ILC2 activation. Therapeutic implications for current and emerging biologic agents.

title: Component-Resolved Diagnostics in Clinical Allergy Practice
abstract: Workshop slides on integrating component-resolved diagnostics into everyday allergy practice. Step-by-step algorithm for interpreting ISAC/ALEX2 results, identifying genuine vs cross-reactive sensitisation, and using CRD findings to guide immunotherapy selection. Case-based interactive format.

title: Perioperative Anaphylaxis: Identification, Management and Follow-Up
abstract: Anaesthesiology symposium slides covering recognition and grading of perioperative hypersensitivity reactions, immediate epinephrine-first management algorithm, and structured allergy investigation protocol post-reaction. Frequency and trigger allergen data from French REMA registry included.

title: Understanding Food Allergy Labels: Patient Education Slide Deck
abstract: Patient-facing education slide deck explaining mandatory allergen declarations, precautionary allergen labelling (may contain, made in a facility with), ingredient list navigation, and strategies for eating safely in restaurants. Available in 8 languages. Designed for use in allergy clinic consultations.

title: SCIT vs SLIT: Comparative Efficacy and Patient Selection
abstract: Evidence-based comparison of subcutaneous (SCIT) and sublingual (SLIT) allergen immunotherapy for rhinitis and asthma. Meta-analysis data on comparative efficacy: SCIT shows marginally superior symptom score reductions for house dust mite; SLIT has better adherence profile. Patient selection algorithm presented.

title: Biologic Pipeline in Allergic Disease: 2024 Update
abstract: Comprehensive pipeline review covering approved and investigational biologics in allergy. Phase III readouts for amlitelimab (anti-OX40L), astegolimab (anti-IL-33), and ordesekimab (anti-IL-31). Positioning within treatment algorithms and key differentiating data discussed.

title: Climate Change and Allergy: Public Health Implications
abstract: WHO-commissioned symposium slides addressing the impact of climate change on allergic disease burden. Extended pollen seasons, increased allergen potency, range expansion of allergenic species, and air quality effects. Policy recommendations for surveillance, adaptation, and prevention at national and global levels.

title: HAE Attack Management: Emergency Department Protocol
abstract: Emergency department protocol slide deck for recognition and management of hereditary angioedema attacks. Differentiates HAE from allergic angioedema and ACE inhibitor angioedema. Treatment algorithm: on-demand C1-INH concentrate and icatibant as first-line therapies. Laryngeal attack emergency escalation pathway included.

title: Precision Allergy Testing: From Theory to Bedside Practice
abstract: Workshop slides translating precision allergy concepts to clinical practice. Integrated diagnostic algorithm combining skin prick testing, specific IgE, basophil activation test, and component-resolved diagnostics. Case presentations from food, pollen, drug, and venom allergy with staged diagnostic approaches.

title: Occupational Asthma: Diagnosis, Management and Medico-Legal Aspects
abstract: Comprehensive occupational asthma slide deck covering case definition, causative agents, specific inhalation challenge (SIC) as gold standard, and management including workplace removal vs retention strategies. Medico-legal aspects of compensation claims and employer duty of care across European jurisdictions.

title: Alpha-Gal Syndrome: Recognition, Diagnosis and Dietary Avoidance
abstract: Clinical education slides on alpha-gal syndrome for allergists and primary care physicians. Covers tick bite mechanism, delayed reaction pattern, diagnostic algorithm (IgE to alpha-gal and Bos d component), and detailed dietary avoidance guidance for mammalian meat and by-products including hidden sources.

title: Paediatric Asthma Action Plan Development Workshop
abstract: Interactive workshop slides for healthcare providers on developing personalised asthma action plans for children and adolescents. Green-yellow-red zone system, symptom-based vs peak flow-based approaches, school and childcare communication templates, and carer training modules included.

title: Drug Desensitisation Protocols: Hospital Pharmacy and Allergy Collaboration
abstract: Protocol development slide deck for rapid drug desensitisation across oncology (platinum compounds, taxanes), rheumatology (biologics), and aspirin desensitisation for AERD. Hospital pharmacy integration model presented. Case series from Brigham and Women's 12-step desensitisation programme.

title: Eosinophil Biology in Type 2 Inflammatory Disease
abstract: Translational science slides reviewing eosinophil biology from development to effector functions in allergic disease. IL-5 signalling pathway, eosinophil tissue homing, and granule protein release mechanisms. Clinical implications for anti-IL-5 and anti-IL-5Ra biologic targeting in asthma, EoE, and HES.

title: Skin Barrier and Allergy Prevention: From Bench to Clinic
abstract: Translational research slides connecting filaggrin biology, skin barrier dysfunction, and clinical prevention strategies. BEEP trial emollient data reappraised in context of newer mechanistic understanding. Current evidence for emollient choice, application technique, and timing in the STOP-AD prevention programme.

title: Omalizumab Across Indications: 20 Years of Clinical Evidence
abstract: 20-year retrospective on omalizumab clinical evidence spanning its approved indications: moderate-to-severe allergic asthma, chronic spontaneous urticaria, nasal polyps, and IgE-mediated food allergy (most recent). Real-world effectiveness data, safety registry findings, and emerging pipeline indications reviewed.

title: Future of Allergy: Gene Therapy, Tolerance Induction and Curative Approaches
abstract: Visionary plenary slides exploring curative approaches to allergic disease beyond symptom management. mRNA-based allergen immunotherapy platforms, CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing of IgE switch regions, regulatory T-cell expansion protocols, and oral tolerance induction with nanoparticle adjuvants. Timelines for clinical translation presented.
